title: Delicate Kenyan Chicken Pilaf
description: A light, herb-infused post-workout chicken pilaf with chervil and aromatic spices, cooked in a clay pot for a pillowy finish. Perfect for a Mediterranean-friendly, protein-packed meal.
instructions: Heat the clay pot over medium heat and drizzle the olive oil.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the cubed chicken thighs, season with salt, pepper, dried chervil, and lemon zest, and cook until the chicken is lightly browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Pour in the chicken stock and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Stir in the basmati rice, cover the clay pot, and reduce the heat to low.
Let the pilaf steam for 18 to 20 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Remove the pot from heat and let it sit, covered, for 5 minutes to allow the grains to fluff.
Fluff the rice with a fork, taste and adjust seasoning if necessary, then serve hot.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
